location: Wharfside Manor picnic area, Monmouth Beach NJ

episodes.title: Light Your First Charcoal Fire
episodes.objective: Show a total beginner the no-fear chimney-starter method: fill the chimney, newspaper underneath, light, wait ~15 min for gray ash, dump. Kill the single biggest beginner fear.
takeaways: A chimney starter is the easiest, no-fluid way to light charcoal
Fill the top with charcoal, crumple newspaper underneath, light the paper
Wait ~15 minutes — walk away, the chimney does the work
Ready when the top coals turn gray and ashy; dump and spread

pages.framing: Bob standing beside the SF163.5 grill, water and gulls behind him, handheld slightly loose for a warm feel, eyeline to lens
pages.action: Bob holds up an empty chimney starter, gives it a friendly little shake
pages.line: If the only grill you've ever used is the gas one at home, lighting charcoal looks scary. It's not — watch me once.
pages.notes: Cold open hook. Lav mic + deadcat on Bob. Golden hour. This is a BLOCK E to-camera wraparound.

pages.framing: Title card slide — wave mark + 'Bob's Grill School', lower-third with episode title
pages.action: Title card: episode title appears over a still of the grill
pages.onScreenText: Bob's Grill School — Ep 1: Light Your First Charcoal Fire
pages.notes: Built in post. End card and title cards are BLOCK F / graphics.

pages.framing: Tripod locked off, Bob at the firebox, chimney on the utility shelf, 3/4 angle showing his hands
pages.action: Bob holds the chimney up, then fills the top basket with charcoal briquettes
pages.line: This metal cylinder is a chimney starter — best three bucks you'll spend. Fill the top with charcoal.
pages.notes: Lock the tripod for the demo. BLOCK A.

pages.framing: Insert, tight on the bottom of the chimney sitting on the grate, hands in frame
pages.action: Bob crumples two sheets of newspaper and stuffs them into the bottom chamber under the chimney
pages.line: Crumple a couple sheets of newspaper underneath — not packed tight, just loose enough to breathe.
pages.notes: Critical cut-in #1. BLOCK A insert.

pages.framing: Tight on the side vent holes of the chimney, long lighter entering frame
pages.action: Bob touches a long lighter to the newspaper through the side holes; paper catches
pages.line: Light the paper through these holes … and walk away. The chimney does the work.
pages.notes: Critical cut-in #2 — the ignition. BLOCK B. Get the flame catching on camera.

pages.framing: Top-down insert over the chimney mouth, locked tripod or high handheld; capture the transition over time
pages.action: Time-lapse-style: coals go from black to glowing orange to gray-ashed at the top
pages.line: About fifteen minutes later — see how the top coals are turning gray and ashy? That means they're ready.
pages.notes: THE money shot. Vertical 9:16 grab for Shorts. Shoot a real timelapse or grab start + end and speed-ramp in post. BLOCK B.

pages.framing: Tripod, Bob with gloves, side angle so the dump arc reads clearly against the water
pages.action: Bob lifts the hot chimney by the handle and dumps glowing coals into the firebox, then spreads them with tongs
pages.line: Grab the handle — use the glove, it's hot — dump them in, spread them out, and you're grilling.
pages.onScreenText: Use the glove — the handle gets HOT
pages.notes: Safety beat baked in (glove). BLOCK C. Great wide dump shot.

pages.framing: Handheld, Bob to camera, glowing firebox in foreground
pages.action: Bob gestures to the ready fire, easy smile
pages.line: That's the whole trick. No fluid, no chemical taste — just a clean fire in fifteen minutes.
pages.notes: BLOCK E to-camera. Can batch with other CTAs.

pages.framing: End card slide over a beauty still of glowing coals
pages.action: End card: CTA and URL
pages.line: Every step's written out at picnic dot vistter dot com. Go light one.
pages.notes: End card. VO can be recorded in BLOCK E. BLOCK F graphic.

episodes.title: Is It Ready? Reading Your Coals
episodes.objective: Teach beginners to read coal readiness by eye (gray ash, not flames), run the 3-second hand heat-test, and rake a two-zone fire with a hot side and a cool 'safe zone'.
takeaways: Ready = glowing and covered in gray ash, NOT shooting flames
No thermometer? The 3-second hand test, 5 inches over the grate
Build a two-zone fire: a hot side to cook, a cool side to retreat to
The cool 'safe zone' is your flare-up insurance

pages.framing: Handheld, Bob to camera, ready firebox glowing beside him
pages.action: Bob points down at the gray coals
pages.line: Flames don't mean ready. THIS — gray and glowing — this means ready.
pages.notes: Cold open hook. BLOCK E to-camera, but needs the gray fire in frame — shoot during BLOCK C if you want it live, else clean to camera and cut in 2B.

pages.framing: Title card slide, wave mark + episode title
pages.action: Title card
pages.onScreenText: Bob's Grill School — Ep 2: Is It Ready? Reading Your Coals
pages.notes: Graphic. BLOCK F.

pages.framing: Top-down insert of the coal bed, tripod, fill the frame with coals
pages.action: Slow push or hold on glowing coals fully covered in gray ash
pages.line: When most of the coals are glowing and covered in gray ash — about fifteen minutes in — you're good to go.
pages.notes: Beauty insert of readiness. BLOCK C / F.

pages.framing: Side angle, tripod, Bob's hand held flat ~5 inches over the grate, his face just in frame
pages.action: Bob holds his palm over the coals, counts on his fingers, pulls back
pages.line: No thermometer? Hold your hand five inches up and count. Pull back at 'three Mississippi' — that's a hot fire.
pages.onScreenText: Hand test: pull back at 3 Mississippi = hot & ready
pages.notes: THE teach moment + vertical 9:16 grab. Make the count readable. BLOCK C.

pages.framing: Tripod, 3/4 over-the-firebox so you see coals move to one side
pages.action: Bob rakes most of the coals to one side with long tongs, leaving the other side bare
pages.line: Now push most of the coals to one side. A hot zone to cook over — and a cool side to slide food to if it flares up.
pages.onScreenText: Two-zone fire = hot side + cool 'safe zone'
pages.notes: Defines the term on screen per tone rule. BLOCK C.

pages.framing: Handheld, Bob to camera, two-zone fire visible below
pages.action: Bob gives a thumbs up over the set fire
pages.line: That's the one trick that turns a scary fire into one you control.
pages.notes: Safety/confidence beat. BLOCK E to-camera.

pages.framing: End card slide over a still of the two-zone fire
pages.action: End card
pages.line: Now you've got a fire you can control. Full steps at picnic dot vistter dot com — let's cook.
pages.notes: End card. BLOCK F.

episodes.title: Burgers & Dogs: Your First Cook
episodes.objective: Walk a first-timer through cooking burgers and dogs to safe temps with confidence: dimple the patties, season just before, flip ONCE and don't press, burgers to 160°F, dogs hot through, thermometer kills the guesswork.
takeaways: Dimple the patties so they don't puff; season just before they hit the grate
Flip ONCE — never press the burgers (you squeeze the juice out)
Burgers to 160°F, dogs just need color and blisters
A $12 instant-read thermometer removes all the guessing

pages.framing: Tight on burgers sizzling on the grate, steam/smoke rising, then tilt up to Bob
pages.action: Burgers searing; Bob leans into frame
pages.line: If you can cook a burger, you can grill. Let's do your first cook — and do it right.
pages.notes: Cold open hook over sizzle. BLOCK D + Bob to-camera line.

pages.framing: Title card slide
pages.action: Title card
pages.onScreenText: Bob's Grill School — Ep 3: Burgers & Dogs, Your First Cook
pages.notes: Graphic. BLOCK F.

pages.framing: Insert over the utility shelf / prep board, Bob's hands forming patties
pages.action: Bob forms a loose patty, presses a dimple into the center with his thumb, salts and peppers both sides
pages.line: Loose patties — don't overwork them. Press a dimple in the middle so they don't puff into a meatball. Salt and pepper right before they hit the grate.
pages.onScreenText: Dimple the center · season just before grilling
pages.notes: Prep insert. Can be shot tableside while coals hold. BLOCK D.

pages.framing: Tripod, 3/4 over the grate, patties laid over the hot zone
pages.action: Bob lays patties and dogs over the coals; sets a timer mentally; hands off
pages.line: Burgers over the hot side, dogs over the cooler side. Now — leave them alone.
pages.notes: BLOCK D. Establishes two-zone use from Ep 2.

pages.framing: Tight on the spatula under a patty, the single flip, tripod
pages.action: Bob slides the spatula under and flips each burger ONCE; resists pressing; adds cheese
pages.line: Resist the urge to flip and press. Once — that's it. Pressing just squeezes the juice out. Cheese in the last minute.
pages.notes: THE critical move + vertical 9:16 grab. Get the single clean flip. BLOCK D.

pages.framing: Insert, instant-read probe going into the side of a burger, display readable to lens
pages.action: Bob probes a burger; thermometer reads 160°F; rolls the dogs showing blisters
pages.line: Burgers to one-sixty. Dogs just need color and a few blisters. A twelve-dollar thermometer takes out all the guessing.
pages.notes: Get the 160°F readout CLEAR on camera — the safety beat. BLOCK D.

pages.framing: Tight on a finished burger built on a toasted bun, hero food shot
pages.action: Bob builds the burger on a toasted bun, lifts it to camera
pages.line: Toast those buns, build it up — congratulations, you're a griller.
pages.notes: Hero plating shot. BLOCK D / F.

pages.framing: End card slide over the hero burger still
pages.action: End card
pages.line: Full cook steps and safe temps at picnic dot vistter dot com.
pages.notes: End card. BLOCK F.

episodes.title: Lighter Fluid, the Safe Way
episodes.objective: Show the safe lighter-fluid method: fluid on UNLIT coals only, wait a minute for it to soak, light from the side. Drill the cardinal rule — NEVER add fluid to lit coals.
takeaways: Lighter fluid goes on UNLIT coals only
Squirt, then WAIT about a minute for it to soak in
Light from the side, never leaning over the pile
NEVER add fluid to lit or smoldering coals — it can flare back at you

pages.framing: Handheld, Bob to camera holding a can of lighter fluid, unlit grill beside him
pages.action: Bob holds up the fluid can
pages.line: Prefer lighter fluid? It's fine — if you do it the safe way. There's one rule that really matters.
pages.notes: Cold open hook. NEEDS A FRESH UNLIT GRILL. BLOCK A + to-camera.

pages.framing: Title card slide
pages.action: Title card
pages.onScreenText: Bob's Grill School — Ep 4: Lighter Fluid, the Safe Way
pages.notes: Graphic. BLOCK F.

pages.framing: Tripod, 3/4 over the firebox, pile of plain unlit briquettes
pages.action: Bob squirts lighter fluid evenly over the UNLIT pile of charcoal
pages.line: Pile your plain charcoal, then squirt the fluid over the cold, unlit pile — a few seconds, not the whole can.
pages.notes: Critical demo on a fresh unlit grill. BLOCK A.

pages.framing: Insert on the pile, Bob's hand setting the can down and away
pages.action: Bob caps the can, sets it well away from the grill, waits
pages.line: Now wait about a minute — let it soak in. And set the can far away before you ever bring a flame near.
pages.onScreenText: Wait ~1 min · move the can away
pages.notes: Safety detail — can away from flame. BLOCK A.

pages.framing: Side angle, tripod, Bob lighting from the EDGE of the pile, body back
pages.action: Bob lights a long match/lighter at the side of the pile; it catches
pages.line: Light it from the side — never leaning over the top. Let it catch and burn down to gray, same as always.
pages.notes: The ignition. BLOCK B. Keep Bob's body back from the flare.

pages.framing: Handheld, Bob to camera, very deliberate, fire going behind him — the SAFETY beat
pages.action: Bob holds up a flat hand, firm but warm, delivers the cardinal rule
pages.line: Here's the one that matters: NEVER add more fluid once the coals are lit — even if they look out. The flame can run right up the stream at you.
pages.onScreenText: NEVER add fluid to lit coals
pages.notes: THE safety beat + vertical 9:16 grab. Most important 'don't' in the series. BLOCK E / B.

pages.framing: End card slide
pages.action: End card
pages.line: Honestly? A chimney starter skips all this. But if you use fluid — now you'll use it safe. picnic dot vistter dot com.
pages.notes: End card. BLOCK F. VO recorded in BLOCK E.

episodes.title: Beat the Wind on the Waterfront
episodes.objective: Show how to handle wind off the river using the grill's 360° rotation and body position so the fire lights and burns steady. Re-spin when the wind shifts.
takeaways: Our SF163.5 grills rotate a full 360° on the pedestal — use it
Turn the firebox so YOUR back is to the wind when you light
Out of the wind it lights faster and burns more evenly
Wind shifts? Just spin the grill again

pages.framing: Handheld, wind visibly moving Bob's shirt/hair, water and whitecaps behind, lighter struggling
pages.action: Bob shields a lighter flame from the wind, it keeps blowing out
pages.line: That river breeze? Gorgeous — until you're trying to light a fire. Let me show you the fix.
pages.notes: Cold open hook — sell the wind. Shoot when it's actually breezy if possible. BLOCK A / E. Lav mic critical here.

pages.framing: Title card slide
pages.action: Title card
pages.onScreenText: Bob's Grill School — Ep 5: Beat the Wind on the Waterfront
pages.notes: Graphic. BLOCK F.

pages.framing: Tripod, wide enough to see the firebox swing on its pedestal, Bob's hands on the rim
pages.action: Bob grips the firebox and rotates the whole head a smooth 180°+ on the pedestal
pages.line: These grills spin a full three-sixty on the pedestal. Turn the firebox so YOU block the wind — your back to the gust.
pages.onScreenText: 360° rotation — put your back to the wind
pages.notes: THE signature move of this episode + vertical 9:16 grab. Make the rotation obvious. BLOCK A.

pages.framing: Behind/over Bob's shoulder so we see him shielding the firebox with his body, OTS
pages.action: Bob, back to the wind, lights the coals successfully on the first try
pages.line: Out of the wind, it lights first try and burns even — no hot spots, no ash blowing in your food.
pages.notes: Payoff shot. BLOCK B — wind-blocked lighting. Pairs with whichever fire you light here.

pages.framing: Tripod, Bob re-gripping and re-spinning the grill as he glances at the water
pages.action: Bob feels the wind shift, re-spins the grill head to keep his back to it
pages.line: Wind shifts during your cook? Don't fight it — spin the grill again. That's the whole trick.
pages.notes: Can shoot over the cooking fire (BLOCK D) or the lit fire. BLOCK D/B.

pages.framing: Handheld, Bob to camera, steady fire and water behind him
pages.action: Bob gestures to the steady fire, relaxed
pages.line: The water's the best part of grilling down here — a little spin and the wind's on your side.
pages.notes: Confidence/close beat. BLOCK E to-camera.

pages.framing: End card slide over a beauty still of the grill + water
pages.action: End card
pages.line: More waterfront grilling tips at picnic dot vistter dot com. Now go enjoy that breeze.
pages.notes: End card. BLOCK F.

gearReminder: Lav mic on Bob + deadcat windscreen — NON-NEGOTIABLE on the waterfront (river wind will wreck on-camera audio)
Grab 2–3 min of clean ambient shore/gull sound separately for the audio bed
Tripod — lock it off for all demo/insert shots; handheld only for hooks & to-camera CTAs
Chimney starter, 2+ bags charcoal briquettes, newspaper, long lighter/matches, long tongs, spatula, heat gloves
Can of lighter fluid (Ep 4 only) + instant-read thermometer (Ep 3, must show 160°F clearly)
Test food: burgers (80/20, pre-formed or form on site), good hot dogs, buns, cheese, plate (Ep 3)
Reflector OR shoot golden hour to avoid raccoon-eye shadows; confirm 10 ft clearance per picnic rules
Heat gloves + sand bucket/water within reach; never leave coals unattended (Wharfside rule)
shotList.shootingOrderNote: A charcoal fire has a one-way lifecycle (unlit → lighting → gray-ready → cooking → cooldown), so plan around 3 fires, not 5 relights. CRITICAL: you cannot demo lighter fluid (Ep 4) on coals already lit by a chimney (Ep 1), and the wind-lighting (Ep 5) needs an unlit firebox too. FIRE 1 (chimney): shoot Ep 1 lighting through gray-ready, then ride that same fire straight into Ep 2 readiness/hand-test/two-zone AND Ep 3 burgers & dogs, AND grab Ep 5's 're-spin during cook' beat over it — one fire covers Blocks A(Ep1)/B(Ep1)/C/D. FIRE 2 (lighter fluid, fresh unlit grill): shoot all of Ep 4 (Block A fluid-on-unlit → Block B side-light → safety beat). FIRE 3 (wind, fresh unlit grill — ideally a genuinely breezy moment): shoot Ep 5's back-to-wind setup and wind-blocked lighting. Batch ALL to-camera hooks/CTAs (Block E) and B-roll/end cards (Block F) separately — no fire required — so a flat shot doesn't cost you a fire. Net: 3 fires, ~2 relights. Tip: light Fire 2 and Fire 3 at adjacent grills back-to-back to parallelize.

shots.framing: Tripod locked, 3/4 on firebox, chimney on shelf
shots.action: Bob fills the chimney top with charcoal
shots.line: Best three bucks you'll spend—fill the top with charcoal.
shots.dependsOn: FIRE 1 — fresh unlit grill (chimney)

shots.framing: Insert, bottom of chimney on grate, hands in frame
shots.action: Crumples newspaper loosely under the chimney
shots.line: Crumple a couple sheets underneath—loose enough to breathe.
shots.dependsOn: FIRE 1 — same unlit grill, before lighting

shots.framing: Handheld, Bob to camera w/ fluid can, unlit grill beside
shots.action: Holds up the lighter-fluid can
shots.line: Prefer fluid? Fine—if you do it the safe way.
shots.dependsOn: FIRE 2 — separate FRESH unlit grill (cannot reuse a lit chimney fire)

shots.framing: Tripod, 3/4 over firebox, plain unlit briquette pile
shots.action: Squirts fluid over the UNLIT pile
shots.line: Squirt it on the cold, unlit pile—a few seconds, not the whole can.
shots.dependsOn: FIRE 2 — same fresh unlit grill

shots.framing: Insert, hand setting the can away from the grill
shots.action: Caps the can, sets it well clear, waits
shots.line: Wait about a minute—let it soak. Set the can far away.
shots.dependsOn: FIRE 2 — before lighting

shots.framing: Handheld, wind moving shirt/hair, water behind
shots.action: Shields a lighter; it keeps blowing out
shots.line: That river breeze? Gorgeous—until you light a fire.
shots.dependsOn: FIRE 3 — separate fresh unlit grill, ideally a breezy moment

shots.framing: Tripod, wide enough to see firebox swing on pedestal
shots.action: Rotates the grill head 180°+ on its pedestal
shots.line: They spin a full 360—turn the firebox so YOU block the wind.
shots.dependsOn: FIRE 3 — same fresh unlit grill (rotation demo before lighting)

shots.framing: Tight on chimney side vents, long lighter entering
shots.action: Lights the newspaper through the side holes
shots.line: Light the paper through these holes… and walk away.
shots.dependsOn: FIRE 1 — ignition (start the 15-min clock here)

shots.framing: Top-down over chimney mouth, capture over time
shots.action: Coals go black → orange → gray-ashed (timelapse)
shots.line: See the tops turning gray and ashy? That means ready.
shots.dependsOn: FIRE 1 — the 15-min burn; shoot start+end, ramp in post

shots.framing: Side angle, tripod, Bob lighting EDGE of pile, body back
shots.action: Lights from the side of the fluid-soaked pile; catches
shots.line: Light it from the side—never over the top.
shots.dependsOn: FIRE 2 — ignition after the 1-min soak

shots.framing: Handheld, Bob to camera, fire behind — SAFETY beat
shots.action: Flat-hand cardinal-rule delivery
shots.line: NEVER add more fluid once it's lit—flame runs up the stream.
shots.dependsOn: FIRE 2 — over the just-lit fluid fire

shots.framing: Over Bob's shoulder, body shielding the firebox
shots.action: Back to wind, lights first try
shots.line: Out of the wind it lights first try and burns even.
shots.dependsOn: FIRE 3 — wind-blocked ignition

shots.framing: Tripod, side angle, gloved dump arc against water
shots.action: Dumps glowing coals into firebox, spreads with tongs
shots.line: Use the glove—it's hot—dump them in, spread out, you're grilling.
shots.dependsOn: FIRE 1 — chimney top gone gray (~15 min after 1E)

shots.framing: Handheld, Bob to camera, ready firebox glowing beside
shots.action: Points down at the gray coals
shots.line: Flames don't mean ready. THIS means ready.
shots.dependsOn: FIRE 1 — needs gray fire in frame (or shoot clean + cut in 2C)

shots.framing: Top-down insert, frame full of coals
shots.action: Hold on glowing, fully gray-ashed coal bed
shots.line: Glowing and covered in gray ash—you're good to go.
shots.dependsOn: FIRE 1 — readiness beauty insert

shots.framing: Side angle, palm ~5in over grate, face in frame
shots.action: Hand test—counts, pulls back at 3 Mississippi
shots.line: Hold your hand five inches up—pull back at 3 Mississippi.
shots.dependsOn: FIRE 1 — hot ready fire

shots.framing: Tripod, 3/4 over firebox
shots.action: Rakes coals to one side—hot zone + bare cool side
shots.line: Hot zone to cook, cool side to slide food to if it flares.
shots.dependsOn: FIRE 1 — builds two-zone (do BEFORE Ep 3 cook)

shots.framing: Insert over prep board, Bob's hands
shots.action: Forms loose patty, thumb-dimples center, seasons
shots.line: Dimple the middle so they don't puff; season just before.
shots.dependsOn: FIRE 1 — can prep tableside while two-zone holds

shots.framing: Tripod, 3/4 over grate
shots.action: Lays burgers on hot zone, dogs on cool zone
shots.line: Burgers over the hot side, dogs over the cooler side—leave them.
shots.dependsOn: FIRE 1 — two-zone fire from 2E

shots.framing: Tight on spatula, the single flip, tripod
shots.action: Flips each burger ONCE, no press, adds cheese
shots.line: Once—that's it. Pressing squeezes the juice out.
shots.dependsOn: FIRE 1 — mid-cook

shots.framing: Insert, probe in side of burger, display to lens
shots.action: Probes burger → 160°F; rolls dogs showing blisters
shots.line: Burgers to 160; dogs just need color. Thermometer ends the guessing.
shots.dependsOn: FIRE 1 — get the 160°F readout clean

shots.framing: Tight on sizzling burgers, tilt up to Bob
shots.action: Burgers searing; Bob leans in
shots.line: If you can cook a burger, you can grill.
shots.dependsOn: FIRE 1 — cooking sizzle (cold-open hook)

shots.framing: Tight hero shot, finished burger on toasted bun
shots.action: Builds the burger, lifts to camera
shots.line: Build it up—congratulations, you're a griller.
shots.dependsOn: FIRE 1 — end of cook (plating/hero)

shots.framing: Tripod, Bob re-gripping and re-spinning the grill
shots.action: Feels wind shift, re-spins to keep back to wind
shots.line: Wind shifts? Don't fight it—spin the grill again.
shots.dependsOn: FIRE 1 or 3 — 'during cook' re-spin; grab over any lit/cooking fire

blocks.title: To-camera wraparounds (hooks + CTAs — batch clean, no fire needed)

shots.framing: Handheld, water + gulls behind, eyeline to lens
shots.action: Shakes an empty chimney, cold-open hook
shots.line: Lighting charcoal looks scary. It's not—watch me once.
shots.dependsOn: No fire needed (empty/unlit grill as backdrop)

shots.framing: Handheld, Bob to camera, fire OR clean
shots.action: Gestures to the fire, CTA recap
shots.line: That's the whole trick—clean fire in fifteen minutes.
shots.dependsOn: Best over FIRE 1 ready fire; can fake clean

shots.framing: Handheld, Bob to camera
shots.action: Thumbs up over the set fire
shots.line: That's the trick that turns a scary fire into one you control.
shots.dependsOn: Best over FIRE 1 two-zone; can batch clean

shots.framing: Handheld, Bob to camera (safety beat backup, no fire)
shots.action: Flat-hand cardinal rule (clean backup of 4F)
shots.line: NEVER add fluid to lit coals—flame runs right up at you.
shots.dependsOn: Optional clean backup of 4F if the fire take is unusable

shots.framing: Handheld, Bob to camera, water behind
shots.action: Gestures to steady fire, relaxed close
shots.line: A little spin and the wind's on your side.
shots.dependsOn: Best over a lit fire; can batch clean

shots.framing: Audio only — record all five end-card VO lines clean on the lav
